## prompt-draft-framework

*Generated by Process A from the submitted `prompts/prompt-adoption.md`
(SUB-PROMPT SCHEDULE item 2), adapted from the prior build's
`review/sub-prompts/prompt-2-draft-narrative.md` and the table-of-contents and
back-matter conventions of `review/final-narrative`. This build is analogous to,
and does not rewrite, the prior review.*

Write the draft-framework (scaffold) LaTeX into
`law-physical-ai-trials/adoption/draft-framework/`, building on the existing
template in `law-physical-ai-trials/review/template` (preserve its visual
appearance exactly: the `#8B2E3F` accent, Linux Libertine body, the title rule,
the abstract box, and the `L`/`C`/`Y` table column types). The draft is a
scaffold: it lays out the full manuscript structure and, in every body section,
provides bracketed `[DRAFTING INSTRUCTIONS: ...]` that name the exact repository
files and directories that the full-framework and final-framework stages must
process, rather than finished prose.

**Structure (one `sections/*.tex` file per section, each `\input` by
`main.tex`).** Front matter (title page on the template theme, abstract,
keywords, disclaimer); an Introduction that defines the lay terms a clinician
needs (Physical AI, surgical humanoid, VVUQ, verification before generation,
Claude Code and Codex peer review, the ten gates, ACCEPT / ESCALATE / BLOCK,
calibrated trust, automation bias and algorithm aversion) and states the three
bedside questions; a clickable, page-filling `\tableofcontents`; the **eight body
sections** mapped to the CONFIDENCE GOALS, in order:

1. `q1-competence.tex` - Competence (is it actually good at this task, on patients
   like mine; validation evidence; the assurance run).
2. `q2-safety.tex` - Safety (what stops it before it hurts someone; the ten gates;
   the catastrophe-predicate BLOCK).
3. `q3-transparency.tex` - Transparency (can I see why it did that; explainable
   output; the hash-chained audit trail).
4. `q4-oversight.tex` - Oversight (am I still in control; human-in-the-loop; the
   ESCALATE path; the override and stop authority).
5. `q5-equity.tex` - Equity (does it work for the patient in front of me; subgroup
   performance; bias surveillance).
6. `q6-reliability.tex` - Reliability (will it be the same tomorrow; reproducibility;
   drift monitoring; uncertainty quantification).
7. `q7-accountability.tex` - Accountability (who is responsible when it is wrong;
   roles; the signed SOP; the documentation chain).
8. `q8-workflow.tex` - Workflow (does it fit how I actually work; integration;
   burden; the consent process).

Then back matter adapted from `review/final-narrative`: References,
Acknowledgments, Ethical Disclosures, Rights and Permissions, Cite This Article,
and Data Availability, each added to the table of contents with `\phantomsection`.

**Bracketed drafting instructions must name exact sources**, for example: the Bill
and prior-work lineage in `review/references/author_works.bib` (entry
`Kawchak2026HR9510Bill`, DOI 10.5281/zenodo.20619762; the surgical-humanoid,
adverse-event, and patient-journey works); the verification evidence carried in
the prior review (the ten-gate VVUQ suite, the 172 of 172 automated tests); the
colored Mermaid catalog in `adoption/mermaid/`; the clinician-confidence
references in `adoption/references/framework_refs.bib`
(`kohn2000toerr`, `makary2016medical`, `obermeyer2019dissecting`, `topol2019high`,
`fda2021samd`, `asme2018vv40`, and the advocacy-science set). Each body section
names which Mermaid figure slot and which table it will carry in the full stage.

**Figures and tables.** Define the colored TikZ Mermaid primitive in the style
file (the `cfwfig` environment and the themed node styles in the strict palette)
and place at least one labeled figure float or table per section, with captions,
even if the draft renders a labeled placeholder. Tables use
`>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{<width>}` columns summing to `\textwidth`.

One commit each for `main.tex`, the `.sty` style file, `references.bib`, and
`README.md`; one commit per `sections/*.tex` file. Also commit
`prompt-draft-framework.md` (this prompt verbatim under a
`## prompt-draft-framework` heading) and `output-draft-framework.md` (the
framework output). For the second-to-last commit fix all errors across all files;
for the last commit write the directory README and the `draft-framework-LaTeX.zip`
Overleaf bundle and refresh the pull request.

**Formatting (centralize in the `.sty`).** White background; RaggedRight body with
even interword spacing and no right-margin overflow; widow and club penalties at
10000 so no single line is stranded and no one or two word last line is left
behind; correct "SS" into "§"; single hyphens only (no en, em, double, or triple
dashes); `\clearpage`, `\vspace`, and `\hspace` used as a senior author would to
balance pages. The scaffold must compile in Overleaf with pdfLaTeX. Commit and
push in real time; do not hold commits. Do not stall, ask questions, or go into
plan mode.
